title: Police and Sheriff's Patrol Officers
description: Maintain order and protect life and property by enforcing local, tribal, state, or federal laws and ordinances. Perform a combination of the following duties: patrol a specific area; direct traffic; issue traffic summonses; investigate accidents; apprehend and arrest suspects, or serve legal processes of courts. Includes police officers working at educational institutions.

tasks.text: Identify, pursue, and arrest suspects and perpetrators of criminal acts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide for public safety by maintaining order, responding to emergencies, protecting people and property, enforcing motor vehicle and criminal laws, and promoting good community relations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record facts to prepare reports that document incidents and activities.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Render aid to accident survivors and other persons requiring first aid for physical injuries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review facts of incidents to determine if criminal act or statute violations were involved.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Investigate illegal or suspicious activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor, note, report, and investigate suspicious persons and situations, safety hazards, and unusual or illegal activity in patrol area.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Testify in court to present evidence or act as witness in traffic and criminal cases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Relay complaint and emergency-request information to appropriate agency dispatchers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor traffic to ensure motorists observe traffic regulations and exhibit safe driving procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Drive vehicles or patrol specific areas to detect law violators, issue citations, and make arrests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Execute arrest warrants, locating and taking persons into custody.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Patrol and guard courthouses, grand jury rooms, or assigned areas to provide security, enforce laws, maintain order, and arrest violators.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Photograph or draw diagrams of crime or accident scenes and interview principals and eyewitnesses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate complaint and emergency-request information to determine response requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Patrol specific area on foot, horseback, or motorized conveyance, responding promptly to calls for assistance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Investigate traffic accidents and other accidents to determine causes and to determine if a crime has been committed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Verify that the proper legal charges have been made against law offenders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transport or escort prisoners and defendants en route to courtrooms, prisons or jails, attorneys' offices, or medical facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct traffic flow and reroute traffic in case of emergencies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Question individuals entering secured areas to determine their business, directing and rerouting individuals as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Notify patrol units to take violators into custody or to provide needed assistance or medical aid.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place people in protective custody.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Serve statements of claims, subpoenas, summonses, jury summonses, orders to pay alimony, and other court orders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inform citizens of community services and recommend options to facilitate longer-term problem resolution.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate and confiscate real or personal property, as directed by court order.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide road information to assist motorists.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct community programs for all ages concerning topics such as drugs and violence.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Process prisoners, and prepare and maintain records of prisoner bookings and prisoner status during booking and pre-trial process.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Supervise law enforcement staff, such as jail staff, officers, and deputy sheriffs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
